data.body:
CONFIRMED BY FALSIFIABLE EXPERIMENT (not just code reading).

Hypothesis: _run_acli (acli_subprocess.py:206), the chokepoint all reconcile ACLI subprocess calls funnel through (create/edit/transition/label/comment/link/delete via self._run), has no timeout, so a hung acli child blocks indefinitely.

Experiment: pointed _run_acli at a fake acli that blocks 30s (sh -c 'sleep 30'), observed for 10s in a thread.
- CONTROL: subprocess.run(same cmd, timeout=3) raised TimeoutExpired at 3.0s -> the measurement method DOES detect a bound when one exists.
- TEST: _run_acli STILL BLOCKING at 10s on the 30s-hanging child, no exception -> NO internal timeout. H1 CONFIRMED.

Scope (verified honestly): the ONLY subprocess timeout in the reconcile orchestration is reconcile.py:454 timeout=60 — and that is on the LOCAL 'rebar list' ticket-fetch, NOT on any Jira/ACLI call. No pass-level watchdog/SIGALRM exists (grep). The REST path (acli_rest _rest_urlopen_with_retry) IS bounded (10-30s/attempt, 3 attempts) — the gap is purely the subprocess path.

Not a same-store race: reconcile serializes same-machine passes via the advisory pass-lock (__main__.py check_pass_lock/acquire_pass_lock + try/finally release). The earlier 30-min hang came from two RAW-primitive probe runs (which bypass the lock), not from a reconcile race — but it surfaced this real unbounded-subprocess gap. Fix per AC: bounded timeout= + TimeoutExpired handling (kill/reap child, retry within _MAX_ATTEMPTS, clear error if exhausted).
